issues: The draft describes this as the 'Centennial Cup Tier II Western Canadian final playoff game' but it is actually the Abbott Cup (Western Canadian final), not the Centennial Cup. The Centennial Cup is the national championship; this series is the western regional final (Abbott Cup). The OCR text does say 'Centennial Cup Tier II' but the context — 'Western Canadian final' between Richmond and Humboldt — identifies this as the Abbott Cup series.

The draft says 'with teams meeting again in Richmond on subsequent dates' — the OCR specifies 'here tonight and Tuesday', meaning the next games are in Richmond (correct location) but the phrasing should reflect the OCR more accurately.
No clear standalone headline is present in the OCR — the AI-drafted headline 'Humboldt defeats Richmond 2-1 in Centennial Cup series' is fabricated rather than sourced from OCR bold text.
Byline is 'The Canadian Press' — this is a wire dateline/source, not a byline per se, but it is present in the OCR and is acceptable as the attribution.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Humboldt Broncos defeat Richmond Sockeyes 2-1 to tie their best-of-seven Western Canadian final series at 2-2 in Centennial Cup Tier II junior hockey.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Bill McDougall and Brad Bergen each scored once as the Humboldt Broncos defeated the Richmond Sockeyes 2-1 on Saturday night in Centennial Cup Tier II junior hockey, tying the best-of-seven Western Canadian final series at 2-2. Dean Rutledge scored the lone Richmond goal. The two teams were scheduled to meet again in Richmond that evening and on Tuesday.
review.notes: No distinct headline is visible in the OCR — the draft headline appears to be AI-generated rather than sourced from bold text. The article is a short Canadian Press wire brief. The series described is the Abbott Cup (Western Canadian final), though the OCR labels it 'Centennial Cup Tier II Western Canadian final' — corrected descriptions preserve the OCR's own language while accurately reflecting the game result and series context.
